title: ranunculus palustris subsp. macrophyllus
intro: Ranunculus palustris subsp. macrophyllus, also known as the marsh crowfoot, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus palustris subsp. macrophyllus include treating urinary tract infections, reducing inflammation and swelling, and aiding in the treatment of kidney stones. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
